rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
Use only visible identifiers and types from the AADL model in AGREE expressions to avoid diagnostics about missing packages or property sets.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions maintain type consistency and avoid introducing undefined or unqualified references.
Generate AGREE clauses that relate visible inputs to outputs without relying on internal subcomponent paths.
Use named constants and helper functions only when they are explicitly declared or imported in the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks, with type declarations using int, real, or bool.
Assumptions and guarantees should be placed in component type annexes, while assignments are valid in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (e.g., ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
